title: Summa Theologiae I, Question 79, Article 1
text: The intellect is a power of the soul and not the very essence of the soul. To understand belongs properly to an immaterial power.

title: Summa Theologiae I, Question 75, Article 2
text: The human soul is something subsistent. The operation of understanding is performed without a corporeal organ.

title: Summa Theologiae I, Question 76, Article 1
text: The intellectual principle which we call the mind or the intellect is united to the body as its form.

title: Summa Theologiae I, Question 85, Article 2
text: The object of the intellect is the quiddity and nature existing in corporeal matter, though not as existing in this individual matter.

title: Summa Theologiae I, Question 14, Article 1
text: Knowledge implies an immateriality of the knower. A thing is known according as it is in the knower.
